lines.gurmukhi: ਪਿਰ ਬਿਨੁ ਜੋਬਨੁ ਬਾਦਿ ਗਇਅਮੁ ਵਾਢੀ ਝੂਰੇਦੀ ਝੂਰੇਉ ॥੫॥
lines.translit: pir bin joban baad geiam vaadtee jhooredee jhooreo |5|
lines.english: but without my Husband Lord, my youth is slipping away uselessly; I am separated from Him, and I cry out in pain. ||5||

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਿਤੀ ਚਖਉ ਸਾਡੜੇ ਕਿਤੀ ਵੇਸ ਕਰੇਉ ॥
lines.translit: kitee chkhau saaddarre kitee ves kareo |
lines.english: I have tasted many flavors, and worn many robes,

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਚੇ ਸੰਦਾ ਸਦੜਾ ਸੁਣੀਐ ਗੁਰ ਵੀਚਾਰਿ ॥
lines.translit: sache sandaa sadarraa suneeai gur veechaar |
lines.english: I have heard the True Lord's message, contemplating the Guru.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਚੇ ਸਚਾ ਬੈਹਣਾ ਨਦਰੀ ਨਦਰਿ ਪਿਆਰਿ ॥੬॥
lines.translit: sache sachaa baihanaa nadaree nadar piaar |6|
lines.english: True is the home of the True Lord; by His Gracious Grace, I love Him. ||6||

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਉ ਭਾਵਨਿ ਤਉ ਜੇਹੀਆ ਮੂ ਜੇਹੀਆ ਕਿਤੀਆਹ ॥
lines.translit: tau bhaavan tau jeheea moo jeheea kiteeaah |
lines.english: O Lord, You are pleased with those who are like Yourself; there are many more like me.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗਿਆਨੀ ਅੰਜਨੁ ਸਚ ਕਾ ਡੇਖੈ ਡੇਖਣਹਾਰੁ ॥
lines.translit: giaanee anjan sach kaa ddekhai ddekhanahaar |
lines.english: The spiritual teacher applies the ointment of Truth to his eyes, and sees God, the Seer.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਬੂਝੈ ਜਾਣੀਐ ਹਉਮੈ ਗਰਬੁ ਨਿਵਾਰਿ ॥੭॥
lines.translit: guramukh boojhai jaaneeai haumai garab nivaar |7|
lines.english: The Gurmukh comes to know and understand; ego and pride are subdued. ||7||

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਨਾਹੁ ਨ ਵੀਛੁੜੈ ਤਿਨ ਸਚੈ ਰਤੜੀਆਹ ॥੮॥੧॥੯॥
lines.translit: naanak naahu na veechhurrai tin sachai ratarreeaah |8|1|9|
lines.english: O Nanak, the Husband does not separate from those who are imbued with Truth. ||8||1||9||

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾ ਭੈਣਾ ਭਰਜਾਈਆ ਨਾ ਸੇ ਸਸੁੜੀਆਹ ॥
lines.translit: naa bhainaa bharajaaeea naa se sasurreeaah |
lines.english: Neither the sisters, nor the sisters-in-law, nor the mothers-in-law, shall remain.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਚਾ ਸਾਕੁ ਨ ਤੁਟਈ ਗੁਰੁ ਮੇਲੇ ਸਹੀਆਹ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: sachaa saak na tuttee gur mele saheeaah |1|
lines.english: The true relationship with the Lord cannot be broken; it was established by the Lord, O sister soul-brides. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਾਰੂ ਮਹਲਾ ੧ ॥
lines.translit: maaroo mahalaa 1 |
lines.english: Maaroo, First Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰ ਬਿਨੁ ਏਤਾ ਭਵਿ ਥਕੀ ਗੁਰਿ ਪਿਰੁ ਮੇਲਿਮੁ ਦਿਤਮੁ ਮਿਲਾਇ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: gur bin etaa bhav thakee gur pir melim ditam milaae |1| rahaau |
lines.english: Wandering so far without the Guru, I grew weary; now, the Guru has united me in Union with my Husband Lord. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਲਿਹਾਰੀ ਗੁਰ ਆਪਣੇ ਸਦ ਬਲਿਹਾਰੈ ਜਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: balihaaree gur aapane sad balihaarai jaau |
lines.english: I am a sacrifice to my Guru; I am forever a sacrifice to Him.

lines.gurmukhi: ਫੁਫੀ ਨਾਨੀ ਮਾਸੀਆ ਦੇਰ ਜੇਠਾਨੜੀਆਹ ॥
lines.translit: fufee naanee maaseea der jetthaanarreeaah |
lines.english: Aunts, uncles, grandparents and sisters-in-law

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਵਨਿ ਵੰਞਨਿ ਨਾ ਰਹਨਿ ਪੂਰ ਭਰੇ ਪਹੀਆਹ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: aavan vanyan naa rehan poor bhare paheeaah |2|
lines.english: - they all come and go; they cannot remain. They are like boatloads of passengers embarking. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਾਮੇ ਤੈ ਮਾਮਾਣੀਆ ਭਾਇਰ ਬਾਪ ਨ ਮਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: maame tai maamaaneea bhaaeir baap na maau |
lines.english: Uncles, aunts, and cousins of all sorts, cannot remain.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਾਥ ਲਡੇ ਤਿਨ ਨਾਠੀਆ ਭੀੜ ਘਣੀ ਦਰੀਆਉ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: saath ladde tin naattheea bheerr ghanee dareeaau |3|
lines.english: The caravans are full, and great crowds of them are loading up at the riverbank. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਾਚਉ ਰੰਗਿ ਰੰਗਾਵਲੋ ਸਖੀ ਹਮਾਰੋ ਕੰਤੁ ॥
lines.translit: saachau rang rangaavalo sakhee hamaaro kant |
lines.english: O sister-friends, my Husband Lord is dyed in the color of Truth.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਚਿ ਵਿਛੋੜਾ ਨਾ ਥੀਐ ਸੋ ਸਹੁ ਰੰਗਿ ਰਵੰਤੁ ॥੪॥
lines.translit: sach vichhorraa naa theeai so sahu rang ravant |4|
lines.english: She who lovingly remembers her True Husband Lord is not separated from Him again. ||4||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਾ ਧਨ ਕੰਤੁ ਪਛਾਣਿਆ ਸੁਖਿ ਸੁਤੀ ਨਿਸਿ ਡੇਹੁ ॥੫॥
lines.translit: saa dhan kant pachhaaniaa sukh sutee nis ddehu |5|
lines.english: That soul-bride, who knows her Husband Lord, sleeps in peace, night and day. ||5||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਭੇ ਰੁਤੀ ਚੰਗੀਆ ਜਿਤੁ ਸਚੇ ਸਿਉ ਨੇਹੁ ॥
lines.translit: sabhe rutee changeea jit sache siau nehu |
lines.english: All the seasons are good, in which the soul-bride falls in love with the True Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਾਰਿ ਪਵੰਦੜੇ ਡਿਠੁ ਮੈ ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਬੋਹਿਥਿ ਚਾੜਿ ॥੬॥
lines.translit: paar pavandarre dditth mai satigur bohith chaarr |6|
lines.english: I have seen them crossing over there, on the boat of the True Guru. ||6||

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਿਕਨੀ ਲਦਿਆ ਹਿਕਿ ਲਦਿ ਗਏ ਹਿਕਿ ਭਾਰੇ ਭਰ ਨਾਲਿ ॥
lines.translit: hikanee ladiaa hik lad ge hik bhaare bhar naal |
lines.english: Some are getting on board, and some have already set out; some are weighed down with their loads.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਤਣਿ ਕੂਕੇ ਪਾਤਣੀ ਵੰਞਹੁ ਧ੍ਰੁਕਿ ਵਿਲਾੜਿ ॥
lines.translit: patan kooke paatanee vanyahu dhruk vilaarr |
lines.english: At the ferry, the ferryman announces, "O travellers, hurry up and cross over."

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾ ਹਮ ਚੰਗੇ ਆਖੀਅਹ ਬੁਰਾ ਨ ਦਿਸੈ ਕੋਇ ॥
lines.translit: naa ham change aakheeah buraa na disai koe |
lines.english: I am not called good, and I see none who are bad.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਨੀ ਸਚੁ ਵਣੰਜਿਆ ਸੇ ਸਚੇ ਪ੍ਰਭ ਨਾਲਿ ॥੭॥
lines.translit: jinee sach vananjiaa se sache prabh naal |7|
lines.english: Those who deal in Truth, remain with their True Lord God. ||7||

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਹਉਮੈ ਮਾਰੀਐ ਸਚੇ ਜੇਹੜਾ ਸੋਇ ॥੮॥੨॥੧੦॥
lines.translit: naanak haumai maareeai sache jeharraa soe |8|2|10|
lines.english: O Nanak, one who conquers and subdues his ego, becomes just like the True Lord. ||8||2||10||

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਾਰੂ ਮਹਲਾ ੧ ॥
lines.translit: maaroo mahalaa 1 |
lines.english: Maaroo, First Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾ ਜਾਣਾ ਮੂਰਖੁ ਹੈ ਕੋਈ ਨਾ ਜਾਣਾ ਸਿਆਣਾ ॥
lines.translit: naa jaanaa moorakh hai koee naa jaanaa siaanaa |
lines.english: I do not believe that anyone is foolish; I do not believe that anyone is clever.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਦਾ ਸਾਹਿਬ ਕੈ ਰੰਗੇ ਰਾਤਾ ਅਨਦਿਨੁ ਨਾਮੁ ਵਖਾਣਾ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: sadaa saahib kai range raataa anadin naam vakhaanaa |1|
lines.english: Imbued forever with the Love of my Lord and Master, I chant His Name, night and day. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਾਬਾ ਮੂਰਖੁ ਹਾ ਨਾਵੈ ਬਲਿ ਜਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: baabaa moorakh haa naavai bal jaau |
lines.english: O Baba, I am so foolish, but I am a sacrifice to the Name.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤੂ ਕਰਤਾ ਤੂ ਦਾਨਾ ਬੀਨਾ ਤੇਰੈ ਨਾਮਿ ਤਰਾਉ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: too karataa too daanaa beenaa terai naam taraau |1| rahaau |
lines.english: You are the Creator, You are wise and all-seeing. Through Your Name, we are carried across. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਮੂਰਖੁ ਸਿਆਣਾ ਏਕੁ ਹੈ ਏਕ ਜੋਤਿ ਦੁਇ ਨਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: moorakh siaanaa ek hai ek jot due naau |
lines.english: The same person is foolish and wise; the same light within has two names.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮੂਰਖਾ ਸਿਰਿ ਮੂਰਖੁ ਹੈ ਜਿ ਮੰਨੇ ਨਾਹੀ ਨਾਉ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: moorakhaa sir moorakh hai ji mane naahee naau |2|
lines.english: The most foolish of the foolish are those who do not believe in the Name. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰ ਦੁਆਰੈ ਨਾਉ ਪਾਈਐ ਬਿਨੁ ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਪਲੈ ਨ ਪਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: gur duaarai naau paaeeai bin satigur palai na paae |
lines.english: Through the Guru's Gate, the Gurdwara, the Name is obtained. Without the True Guru, it is not received.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਕੈ ਭਾਣੈ ਮਨਿ ਵਸੈ ਤਾ ਅਹਿਨਿਸਿ ਰਹੈ ਲਿਵ ਲਾਇ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: satigur kai bhaanai man vasai taa ahinis rahai liv laae |3|
lines.english: Through the Pleasure of the True Guru's Will, the Name comes to dwell in the mind, and then, night and day, one remains lovingly absorbed in the Lord. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਹੁਕਮੀ ਬਾਧੇ ਪਾਸੈ ਖੇਲਹਿ ਚਉਪੜਿ ਏਕਾ ਸਾਰੀ ॥੪॥
lines.translit: hukamee baadhe paasai kheleh chauparr ekaa saaree |4|
lines.english: Bound by the Hukam of God's Command, the dice are thrown; he is just a piece in the game of chess. ||4||

lines.gurmukhi: ਰਾਜੰ ਰੰਗੰ ਰੂਪੰ ਮਾਲੰ ਜੋਬਨੁ ਤੇ ਜੂਆਰੀ ॥
lines.translit: raajan rangan roopan maalan joban te jooaaree |
lines.english: In power, pleasures, beauty, wealth and youth, one gambles his life away.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਗਿ ਚਤੁਰੁ ਸਿਆਣਾ ਭਰਮਿ ਭੁਲਾਣਾ ਨਾਉ ਪੰਡਿਤ ਪੜਹਿ ਗਾਵਾਰੀ ॥
lines.translit: jag chatur siaanaa bharam bhulaanaa naau panddit parreh gaavaaree |
lines.english: The world is clever and wise, but it is deluded by doubt, and forgets the Name; the Pandit, the religious scholar, studies the scriptures, but he is still a fool.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਉ ਵਿਸਾਰਹਿ ਬੇਦੁ ਸਮਾਲਹਿ ਬਿਖੁ ਭੂਲੇ ਲੇਖਾਰੀ ॥੫॥
lines.translit: naau visaareh bed samaaleh bikh bhoole lekhaaree |5|
lines.english: Forgetting the Name, he dwells upon the Vedas; he writes, but he is confused by his poisonous corruption. ||5||
